title: Homя

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams] [Air Whooshing]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer presumably crashing or falling
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation followed by physical mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase in typical context - perfectly integrated character moment but not innovative

jokes.text: Animation is so great. It's way better than... whatever the alternative is.
jokes.setup: Praising animation as superior entertainment
jokes.punchline: Vague dismissal of live-action as 'whatever the alternative is'
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-humor about the medium itself, good economy but generic delivery

jokes.text: Wait. I'm confused. Why was a wolf shooting a web?
jokes.setup: Watching nonsensical anime-style cartoon
jokes.punchline: Child's logical question about cartoon physics
jokes.explanation: Good child logic applied to absurd cartoon - well-structured observational humor

jokes.text: Cartoons don't have to make sense. He's right, you know.
jokes.setup: Bart questioning cartoon logic
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact dismissal followed by Marge's agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary on The Simpsons' own cartoon logic - excellent economy and character fit

jokes.text: What you making there, Gravey? It's a pipe bomb, Jobriath... for to blow up Planned Parenthood.
jokes.setup: Innocent question about what someone is making
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of making terrorist device for political violence
jokes.explanation: Dark satirical take on religious extremism presented with childlike innocence - edgy for network TV

jokes.text: All I need is some courage. Courage, I tell ya. Excuse me, but isn't that voice very similar to the Cowardly Lion?
jokes.setup: Voice actor performing character voice
jokes.punchline: Reporter immediately calling out obvious plagiarism
jokes.explanation: Standard setup-punchline exposing Hollywood plagiarism - competent but predictable

jokes.text: Humina, humina, humina, humina, humina. And Clutzy Cat sounds exactly like Jerry Lewis... with the stealing and the lawsuit and the 'Oh, nice judge. Don't hurt a person with the thing.'
jokes.setup: Voice actor doing obvious Jackie Gleason impression
jokes.punchline: Reporter's spot-on Jerry Lewis impression with meta-commentary about lawsuits
jokes.explanation: Clever layering of celebrity impression with meta-joke about Hollywood legal issues

jokes.text: Here's your smokes, Mr. Itchy. Thank you, Louis.
jokes.setup: 1950s-style cigarette commercial parody
jokes.punchline: Cartoon characters smoking cigarettes behind the scenes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of wholesome cartoon imagery with dark 1950s advertising reality

jokes.text: I don't know what's in 'em. I just know I can't stop smokin' 'em! [All Laughing] [All Coughing]
jokes.setup: Characters praising addictive cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Immediate transition from laughter to coughing fits
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and structure - the immediate consequence undercuts the false advertising

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Was that cat making out with that mouse? 'Cause if they were-
jokes.setup: Flanders watching Itchy and Scratchy behind-the-scenes footage
jokes.punchline: His prudish concern about cartoon animal romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders character moment - his prudishness applied to absurd situation

jokes.text: Pick me! Pick me!
jokes.setup: Demonstration asking for volunteer
jokes.punchline: Homer's eager volunteer enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer eagerness - perfectly in character but predictable

jokes.text: I'm a little teapot, short and stout. Here is my handle, Here is my spout
jokes.setup: Motion capture demonstration with cartoon character copying movements
jokes.punchline: Homer performing children's song with adult seriousness
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike enthusiasm creates humor through incongruity with his age

jokes.text: I'll make him an offer he can't refuse. And that's the truth. [Raspberry]
jokes.setup: Homer doing impressions for motion capture
jokes.punchline: Mixing Godfather with Lily Tomlin's Edith Ann character plus raspberry
jokes.explanation: Random impression medley shows Homer's scattered pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: Don't spay or neuter your pets. Good night!
jokes.setup: End of Homer's motion capture performance
jokes.punchline: Subverts Bob Barker's famous 'spay and neuter' message
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of familiar TV catchphrase with opposite message - excellent economy

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Urinal cake eroding. Eroding! Eroding! Gone! [All Cheer] [All Whimper]
jokes.setup: Homer narrating mundane bathroom maintenance
jokes.punchline: Crowd cheering then immediately becoming sad when urinal cake dissolves
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor elevating mundane bathroom fixture to dramatic entertainment

jokes.text: An opossum capering around like that would be a smash hit. It would be the world's funniest marsupial.
jokes.setup: Homer getting excited about motion capture technology
jokes.punchline: Grandiose claims about animated opossum's comedy potential
jokes.explanation: Homer's naive enthusiasm and specific marsupial classification creates humor

jokes.text: And I'm gonna get a piece of the action! So, does he still work here, or what?
jokes.setup: Homer declaring his business plan and rushing off
jokes.punchline: Confused question about Homer's employment status
jokes.explanation: Homer's impulsive nature leaving others confused - solid character beat

jokes.text: Hello, Marge. [High-pitched Voice] Hi, Homie. Sign this, please. [High-pitched Voice] You're the boss.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to forge Marge's signature at bank
jokes.punchline: Obvious fake voice impression behind plant
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer scheme with terrible execution - physical comedy implied

jokes.text: [High-pitched Voice] Daddy, ask the man for some candy. No, no. No candy for you. [High-pitched Voice] Well, at least get some candy for yourself. [Chuckles] Kids.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing fake family charade
jokes.punchline: Adding fake child voice and bank teller playing along
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the scam with bank teller's unexpected cooperation

jokes.text: You heard the monkey. Make the trade.
jokes.setup: Stock trading scene with Homer making financial decisions
jokes.punchline: Taking investment advice from a monkey sound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating random monkey noise as financial wisdom

jokes.text: Animotion, up one and one-half. Yahoo! Yahoo, up six and a quarter. Huh? What is this crap?
jokes.setup: Homer calling for stock prices
jokes.punchline: Automated system misunderstanding 'Yahoo!' as company name
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection playing on voice recognition technology and Homer's celebration

jokes.text: After plunging 75 points this morning. Oh. I hope 'plunging' means 'up' and '75' means '200.'
jokes.setup: Bad news about Homer's stock investment
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate reinterpretation of clear language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer denial mechanism - trying to redefine reality through willpower

jokes.text: The firm declared super-duper bankruptcy... which is terrible news for the company's only stockholder, Homer Simpson.
jokes.setup: Regular bankruptcy wasn't bad enough
jokes.punchline: Absurd 'super-duper bankruptcy' plus Homer being only stockholder
jokes.explanation: Childish modifier 'super-duper' makes financial disaster absurdly worse, plus Homer isolation

jokes.text: Okay, people. Let's keep this short. We all want to get home to our families. [All Laughing] All right. First item. I lost our life savings in the stock market.
jokes.setup: Homer treating family meeting like business meeting
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of financial catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition of business formality with personal disaster - great structure

jokes.text: Now let's move on to the real issue- Lisa's hogging of the maple syrup. Well, maybe if Mom didn't make such dry waffles. There, I said it.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing major financial loss
jokes.punchline: Treating syrup complaint as more important, Lisa's brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Good priority inversion plus Lisa's unexpected savage response about Marge's cooking

jokes.text: Well, maybe if you ate some meat, you'd have a natural lubricant.
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Marge's dry waffles
jokes.punchline: Marge's bizarre dietary theory about meat providing lubrication
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly crude comeback from usually innocent Marge - subverts character expectations

jokes.text: Point of order. I didn't lose all the money. There was enough left for this cowbell. Damn you, eBay!
jokes.setup: Homer defending against losing all money accusation
jokes.punchline: Revealing he spent remaining money on useless cowbell via eBay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - technically correct but spectacularly missing the point. eBay blame is perfect

jokes.text: Damn you, eBay!
jokes.setup: Homer defending his cowbell purchase
jokes.punchline: Blaming eBay for his poor financial decisions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer deflection - blame the platform, not his choices. Cultural reference feels natural.

jokes.text: You rent your womb to a rich childless couple. If you agree, signify by getting indignant. Are you crazy? I'm not gonna be a surrogate mother.
jokes.setup: Homer's scheme for making money
jokes.punchline: His backwards logic that indignation means agreement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reverse psychology joke - Homer's twisted logic that anger means consent

jokes.text: It's 'uter-us,' not 'uter-you.'
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince Marge about surrogacy
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun treating 'uterus' as possessive pronoun
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer wordplay - terrible pun delivered with confidence, very quotable

jokes.text: Like you chew on a telephone wire till you get a shock. Oh. Oh, right. Okay.
jokes.setup: Barney explaining his human guinea pig work
jokes.punchline: Casual description of dangerous electrical testing, Homer's acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good Barney character moment - treating dangerous activities as routine work

jokes.text: Are those ears? Ow! Not so loud!
jokes.setup: Homer noticing Barney's unusual anatomy
jokes.punchline: Barney's hypersensitive hearing from ear experiments
jokes.explanation: Good physical consequence of earlier setup about experimental side effects

jokes.text: We'll call it Desert Breeze.
jokes.setup: Homer screaming in pain from experimental perfume
jokes.punchline: Scientists giving it a pleasant name despite obvious torture
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about corporate product naming. The contrast between agony and pleasant marketing name works well.

jokes.text: Food? [Whimpers] I'm blind! [Screaming] Who's gonna buy a pill that makes you blind?
jokes.setup: Homer testing appetite suppressant
jokes.punchline: Drug works too well causing blindness, scientists' casual market concern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of side effects plus scientists' twisted market priorities

jokes.text: He's a little too well fed to be a wino. Hey. What's that? Zoom in on section eight.
jokes.setup: Scientists examining Homer's brain scan
jokes.punchline: Professional assessment that Homer doesn't fit wino demographic
jokes.explanation: Clinical observation about test subject demographics - dry scientific humor

jokes.text: But I've had thousands of head X-rays. How come no one ever noticed it before? Oh, I can answer that. You see, whenever I pick up an X-ray, I always hold it like this. My thumb must have covered up the crayon every time.
jokes.setup: Homer wondering how crayon was never noticed in X-rays
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hibbert's thumb consistently covering the same spot on every X-ray
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist explanation for medical oversight - specific and believable incompetence

jokes.text: [Chuckles] I'll show myself out.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert admitting massive professional negligence
jokes.punchline: His casual self-dismissal after revealing incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Hibbert character beat - maintaining dignity despite massive failure

jokes.text: Fourteen. Fifteen. Sixteen! Whoo-hoo! Oh, I don't feel so good. [Sneezes] Ah, I think that's all of them.
jokes.setup: Flashback to Homer shoving crayons up his nose as a child
jokes.punchline: Counting to sixteen crayons, celebration, then immediate regret
jokes.explanation: Perfect origin story for Homer's stupidity - child's achievement mentality about self-harm

jokes.text: Hey, I came here to be drugged, electrocuted and probed, not insulted.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting Homer has subnormal intelligence
jokes.punchline: Homer's indignant list of acceptable treatments vs. insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - physical torture is fine but don't question his intelligence

jokes.text: Increase my killing power, eh? Let's do it.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining brain surgery could increase brainpower
jokes.punchline: Homer mishearing as 'killing power' and enthusiastically agreeing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer mishearing plus his disturbing enthusiasm about violence - excellent economy

jokes.text: And they gave me this spiffy nerd ensemble too.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his brain surgery results
jokes.punchline: Proudly showing off stereotypical nerd outfit
jokes.explanation: Visual gag with Homer's new look - competent but predictable smart person costume

jokes.text: Dr. Joyce Brothers may be well-known... but her psychological credentials are highly suspect.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to say something smart
jokes.punchline: Highly specific academic criticism of pop psychologist
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of Homer's new intelligence through unexpected intellectual snobbery

jokes.text: Notice I no longer say 'libary' or 'tomorry.'
jokes.setup: Homer's new intelligence affecting his behavior
jokes.punchline: Self-aware correction of his previous mispronunciations
jokes.explanation: Smart callback to established character trait - shows continuity awareness

jokes.text: It's so tragic the way they hopped on Pop.
jokes.setup: Homer discussing reading children's book 'Hop on Pop'
jokes.punchline: Treating children's book as serious tragedy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reinterpretation of innocent children's book as serious literature - unexpected perspective

jokes.text: Now hold still. Nothing cracks a turtle like Leon Uris. Ow! Dang it! Ow!
jokes.setup: Cletus at library 'checking out' a book
jokes.punchline: Using heavy book as physical weapon on turtle, literary pun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus character moment - literal interpretation of 'checking out' plus literary pun

jokes.text: That sonata may not be a Glenn Gould performance... but I must say it's 'good as Gould.' [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Flanders commenting on piano performance
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun on Glenn Gould's name delivered with self-satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders character beat - dad joke delivered with pride in cultural reference

jokes.text: Better. I was working on a flat tax proposal... and I accidentally proved there's no God.
jokes.setup: Flanders asking if Homer found shortcut to church
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual mention of accidentally disproving God's existence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from mundane tax work to theological catastrophe - perfect economy and originality

jokes.text: We'll just see about tha- Uh-oh. Well, maybe he made a mista- No. It's airtight.
jokes.setup: Ned challenging Homer's proof against God
jokes.punchline: His gradual realization that Homer is right
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned reaction showing his faith crisis in real time. Great structure with the escalating realization.

jokes.text: We'll just see about tha- Uh-oh. Well, maybe he made a mista- No. It's airtight. Can't let this little doozy get out.
jokes.setup: Flanders examining Homer's proof against God
jokes.punchline: Progressive realization and acceptance that proof is valid
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure showing Flanders' faith crisis in real time - excellent character work

jokes.text: I am here to give hope to the least of you... because we all have a crayon up our nose. Maybe it's not a crayon made of wax. Maybe it's a crayon made of prejudice.
jokes.setup: Homer giving inspirational speech to students
jokes.punchline: Extending crayon metaphor to societal issues
jokes.explanation: Smart extension of literal plot device into metaphorical social commentary

jokes.text: A moron says what? Not being a moron, I wouldn't know. However- [Mumbles] What? Ladies and gentlemen, I give you your moron.
jokes.setup: Nelson trying classic 'moron says what' trick on smart Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer cleverly avoiding trap then falling for mumbled follow-up
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Homer outsmarts the trick then immediately fails to simpler version

jokes.text: I've compiled a thorough safety report on this plant. I've decided to disintermediate the local authorities... and send it straight to the Nuclear Regulatory Commission.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining what he's mailing
jokes.punchline: Using business buzzword 'disintermediate' for whistleblowing
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of Homer's new vocabulary creating workplace problems

jokes.text: Homer's the guy who rigged up my pants with this special codpiece. Comfy, isn't it? Oh, yeah. It provides the freedom and protection I so sorely need.
jokes.setup: Carl praising Homer's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Homer's engineering applied to medieval genital armor
jokes.explanation: Absurd application of Homer's newfound intelligence to uncomfortable medieval solution

jokes.text: I can't feed my family with a codpiece.
jokes.setup: Plant workers angry about losing jobs due to Homer's report
jokes.punchline: Carl specifically blaming the codpiece for his unemployment
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that connects Homer's inventions to economic consequences

jokes.text: Wait. You can't hate me. I'm your better! Your better!
jokes.setup: Angry workers confronting Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's arrogant claim of superiority repeated for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Homer's new intelligence creating social problems - repetition adds desperation

jokes.text: Hey! That's me! Stop that. The fire inspector would be appalled.
jokes.setup: Workers burning Homer in effigy
jokes.punchline: Homer's bureaucratic concern about fire safety during his own burning
jokes.explanation: Perfect smart Homer moment - prioritizing regulations over personal safety

jokes.text: And I was a lot happier before I knew Dame Edna was a man. A lot happier.
jokes.setup: Barney complaining about Homer making them smarter
jokes.punchline: Specific example of knowledge ruining innocent enjoyment
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of 'ignorance is bliss' theme with specific cultural reference

jokes.text: I'm detecting a distinct strain... of anti-intellectualism in this tavern. Power off, Einstein.
jokes.setup: Homer analyzing the bar's hostility toward intelligence
jokes.punchline: Moe's blunt dismissal of Homer's analysis
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's academic language and Moe's direct response

jokes.text: Oh, many things. Tai chi, chai tea... but I find when I can't keep the unhappy thoughts from swirling in my brain... the best thing is usually a nice long walk.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining how she copes with intelligence
jokes.punchline: Listing pretentious activities then simple solution
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment showing her coping mechanisms for being smart

jokes.text: You know she's gonna wind up marrying Richard Gere. I thought she'd wind up with that rich snob. Ably played by Bill Paxton. It's Bill Pullman, you fool!
jokes.setup: Homer spoiling movie plot for other viewers
jokes.punchline: Argument over Bill Paxton vs. Bill Pullman identity
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about confusing similar actors plus Homer spoiling movies

jokes.text: Hey. Don't blame me. This movie is tired and predictable.
jokes.setup: Homer not laughing at movie with everyone else
jokes.punchline: His intellectual criticism of the film
jokes.explanation: Good example of intelligence isolating Homer from simple pleasures. Meta-commentary on movie criticism.

jokes.text: I'm a Spalding Gray in a Rick Dees world.
jokes.setup: Homer feeling isolated by his intelligence
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to cerebral monologist vs. lowbrow DJ
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural reference contrasting intellectual vs. commercial entertainment - perfect economy

jokes.text: That's ridiculous. You do nothing but play God. And I think your 'octo-parrot' would agree. [Squawks] Polly shouldn't be. [Whistles]
jokes.setup: Scientists claiming they don't play God
jokes.punchline: Homer citing mutant bird as evidence, creature's existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist creation with existential implications - creature questions its own existence

jokes.text: The old 'Crayola oblongata.'
jokes.setup: Moe performing brain surgery on Homer
jokes.punchline: Combining crayon brand name with brain anatomy term
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining brand name with medical terminology - very quotable

jokes.text: Extended warranty? How can I lose? Perfect.
jokes.setup: Moe testing Homer's intelligence during surgery
jokes.punchline: Homer falling for classic sales pitch indicating restored stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect indicator of Homer's return to stupidity - falling for obvious scam

jokes.text: We were what-what in the what-what?
jokes.setup: Lisa saying they were connecting meaningfully
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused repetition showing he's back to being dumb
jokes.explanation: Good confirmation that Homer is back to his old self. The repetition shows his confusion effectively.

jokes.text: Lisa, I'm taking the coward's way out. But before I do, I just want you to know... being smart made me appreciate how amazing you really are.
jokes.setup: Homer's letter explaining his decision to reverse the surgery
jokes.punchline: Heartfelt appreciation for Lisa's daily experience with intelligence
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment that validates Lisa while explaining Homer's choice

jokes.text: Oh, you want a hug. Well, that I know how to do. [Grunts] Mmm. Hug.
jokes.setup: Lisa's emotional reaction to Homer's letter
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple understanding of basic affection needs
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Homer may be dumb but he understands love and comfort

tmdb_overview: While working as a human guinea pig (to pay off the family's lost savings after making a bad investment), Homer discovers the root cause of his subnormal intelligence: a crayon that was lodged in his brain ever since he was a boy. He decides to have it removed to increase his IQ, but discovers that being smart does not necessarily equal being happy.
